text:
This is a lovely park with lots of fountains, food stands, lights, rides, and lots of activities for kids!

There are so many beautiful water features and lights to admire from up close or far away!

For watching the show, I recommend standing in the middle of the long, main fountain area to get the best view, and arrive there 5-10 mins early to get a good spot ✨

text: Circuito Mágico del Agua del Parque de la Reserva, in downtown Lima, is a small theme park centred around thrice-nightly water shows. Such a beautiful park, I
Kept walking past my park as I was walking around the city & every time I looked through & saw the fountains I thought I must go but nothing quite prepared me for just how beautiful it was.

It is a family park so be prepared for lots of family activities & you do pay extra for certain rides, experience & entry but for me it was equivalent of a mini Disney land so the prices were beyond reasonable & the queues were nothing in comparison of what you might expect in European parks with a Sumatra offering in my experience.

I highly recommend spending the day into the evening here so you get the full daylight & evening experience. Do make sure your phone is fully charged as you may want to take 1000 photos 🤣!

Enjoy.

Once inside I was surprised by how busy it was and how much it catered to children. It felt a bit like a hybrid amusement/water/city park of sorts. We still had a good time and enjoyed the history of Peru show that was about 15 minutes long and occurs at 19:15, 20:15, and 21:30 every night except Mondays.

text:
Wow where to begin....
This was such a neat experience! I absolutely loved this park. I seen pictures of the inside, but trust me when I say it doesn't do it any justice! Just the expanse of different fountains and how huge the park is was just mind boggling.
My one advice would be to take a change of clothes and shoes with you (especially if you have small children) there is a play area for them that they can run around and get wet in with a parent nearby. I wanted to have my daughter do it, but we had only tennis shoes for her. Oh well, next time 🤗 it's a perfect excuse to go back right?
Also I know at night they all light up. We seen parts of the big long fountain change colors once it got a bit more into the evening and it was so pretty. If we ever go at night I will have to post those pics for you all. Till then... enjoy these and know this is definitely worth a visit!

text:
This park is arguably the best evening activity to explore in Lima at an affordable price. The entire setup, including the water fountains, the lush green park, the light and sound show and the water tunnel, offers the perfect evening for families and loved ones. The highlight is undoubtedly the show on the water screen, which showcases and elaborates on the history and culture of Peru. It's simply breathtaking, a perfect combination of sound, music, color and images that vividly tell the story of Peru.

Please note that show timings may vary depending on the day and month. The entry ticket price is 4 soles per person.

text:
When I first entered, I was underwhelmed. But after walking around for a while and seeing the different fountains, I realised that there was actually more there than I first anticipated.
The main fountains varied their patterns over time, keeping interest alive. They varied from strong jets of water to spray effects and the lighting added further interest.
The main Magic Fountain has incredibly powerful jets that shoot water up very high at times and the patterns vary over time well. There is also a fun tunnel made from water that people can walk through, but watch out for idiots that like to disrupt the water jets by putting their hands on the way in order to try to wet other visitors.
But the main attraction is the 120 metre Fantasy Fountain, which has a display every 45 minutes or so. During this time, the fountains create a spray onto which projectors display an interesting film about the history of the country. This is mixed with music, lighting and other water jets to very good effect.
It only cost 4 Soles to enter and they accept credit cards also. As you might expect, there are also food and drink vendors and toilets inside. There is also a fountain that people can go inside and play in (and get soaking wet).

text:
If you think it is a fountain, think again. The park is spread over a very large area and has over 12 fountains, each better than the others. You will be taken off guard on seeing the Louvre - made by fountains! A water tunnel is a crowd puller and people walk under it as if in an excited trance. The main fountain, 80 metres tall, changes shapes and sizes and the grand plaza is host to a number of photo sessions. One can spend hours and hours here.
Take a change of clothes if you want to walk among some of the fountains. Many people do so.
One is also amazed at the upkeep of the park and the fountains. All of them are working properly. You can take some lovely pictures here, there are so many picture corners of small and big fountains all over the place.
A wonderful experience, beyond imagination.

text:
Bring your nighttime equipped cameras and recording equipment, this place is truly amazing and whimsical.
The dancing water fountains showcased through lights and music are a joy and spectacle to behold and are sure to bring a smile to your face.
The fountains are spread out in a meandering walkway ( a circuit) that start small and culminate at a majestic grand fountain showcasing a dancing water show of synchronized pageantry.
The entrance fee is extremely affordable and well worth to witness this colorful and exuberant public space.
All large metropolitan cities should offer a grand park like this to it's citizens to fostering genuine family time that is both genuine, fun and inspiring. A jewel of Lima and the beautiful country of Peru.

text:
Must see Lima evening experience for 4 soles!
The 1 hour colourful water fountain displays start at 7pm then again at 9pm.
Suitable for everyone, young and old, from small children, families, romantic couples, teenagers, elderly and the mobility impaired.
If tired of walking with your young children there is a mini train that meanders through the circuit of displays.

I felt safe, there are security people around.

And not to worry about how you are going to get home to your hotels. There are official taxi people wearing large Identity cards waiting at the exit. You may want to barter down your taxi price quote. It was going to cost double more the price of travelling to the show. We bartered down to almost half of his quote.
I would go again next time I am in Lima, such a relaxing visually beautiful evening out.

text:
This is a great place to visit, I would say it is a must if you are visiting Lima. And if you are a resident in the Peruvian capital, it is a must visit every so often.

The Park where the Magic Water Circuit is located is huge (8 hectares) and opens from 3:00 p.m. to 10:30 p.m. It is not only a park for the enjoyment of children, but for the whole family. It is very well preserved, located very centrally, and the prices (tickets) are very accessible: PEN 4.00 per person.

It is a circuit of 13 computer-controlled fountains, which offer a fun, interesting and unique show of music, color, images ... and water. Do not miss it.

The fountain that displays videos on a water screen, has "functions" at 19:15, 20:15 and 21:30, so you should try to be in front of it at that time ... or a little earlier since many of the almost 10,000 daily visitors that the park receives on average are crowded together for the show.
________________________
Este es un gran lugar para visitar, yo diría que imperdible si es que estás de visita en Lima, y si eres residente en la capital peruana, pues es de obligada visita cada cierto tiempo.

El Parque donde está ubicado el Circuito Mágico del Agua es enorme (8 hectáreas) y abre desde la 15:00 hasta las 22:30. No solo es un parque para el disfrute de los niños, sino de toda la familia. Está muy bien conservado, ubicado muy céntricamente, y los precios (entradas) son muy accesibles: PEN 4.00 por persona.

Se trata de un circuito de 13 fuentes controladas por computadora, las cuales ofrecen un divertido, interesante y único espectáculo de música, color, imágenes… y agua. No te lo pierdas.

La fuente que despliega videos en un screen de agua, tiene “funciones” a las 7:15, 8:15 y 9:30, por lo que debes tratar de estar en frente de ella a esas horas… o un poco antes ya que se aglomeran muchos de los casi 10,000 visitantes diarios que el parque recibe en promedio.
address: Jirón Madre de Dios S/N, Lima 15046, Peru
title: Magic Water Circuit of the Reserve Park

text:
Parque de la Reserva is a stunning water park. Entry is free early in the day, and a small charge applies later on. There are 13 major fountains, all of which are impressive and interesting. Three of them are set up for playing in, with interactive features. There are minimum age limits for playing in the fountains (around 11 or so years, but differs for various fountains). Guards will blow their whistles and chase out children who are too young. A pity really, because younger children really enjoy fountain play on a hot day. A separate childrens play area offers inflatable slides and other play equipment for children, but it's at extra cost.

After dusk the fountains are illuminated, some with white light but others with dynamic patterns of coloured lights. It's quite amazing, and some of the fountains have lighting which is quite "over-the-top". In addition there are three special light shows each evening, where animated images are projected onto the largest fountain, with good effect.
